extraction.one_line_summary: JACOBSEN Fabio won the bunch sprint after GAVIRIA Fernando went long from 300m out, fading in final 100m
tactical_patterns: GAVIRIA went early from 300m with 3-bike-length gap, revealing sustained power but insufficient finishing speed for modern sprints.
JAYCO-ALULA did disproportionate chase work despite having GROENEWEGEN, suggesting either leadership ambiguity or domestique obligations from previous agreements.
GIRMAY punctured at 10km but still finished 4th, indicating peloton pace control was unusually moderate in final kilometers.

rider_signals.JACOBSEN Fabio: positive: won with longest acceleration distance of frontrunners, suggesting season form building after poor 2022 start
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: neutral: initiated sprint from distance but lacked terminal velocity, indicating timing judgment issues not power deficit
rider_signals.GIRMAY Biniam: positive: recovered from 10km puncture to 4th place finish, demonstrating superior chase positioning skills
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: negative: team worked extensively but he couldn't contest top positions, suggesting form concerns or leadout coordination failure
